home.manifesto: ORIGINAL INTERIORS THAT STAY FOR LONGER. WE CREATE INTERIORS WITH A DISTINCT IDENTITY. WE COMBINE DESIGN SENSITIVITY WITH PRECISION AND EXPERIENCE, CREATING SPACES REFINED IN DETAIL AND GROUNDED IN REAL CONTEXT.
home.tagline: ORIGINAL INTERIORS THAT STAY FOR LONGER.
home.section1Text: We design spaces that don't rely on trends. We treat each space individually, as an answer to a specific context and needs. Whether it's a residential or commercial project, we'll create an interior that tells your story.
home.section2Text: We have years of experience in delivering projects of various character — from furniture details, through apartments and houses, to gastronomic, commercial and hotel venues. We manage the process comprehensively, with full control over the final result. This is how refined spaces are created, filled with original, unique elements. We are based in Wrocław and run projects across Poland — including Warsaw, Łódź and Gdańsk.

meta.schemaDescription: Wrocław-based interior architecture practice specializing in residential and commercial interiors, custom furniture, lighting design, and visual identity. Projects across Poland, including Warsaw.

studio.title: Studio
studio.manifesto: WE ARE KOOL AND WE DESIGN KOOL THINGS!
studio.intro: A Wrocław-based design studio founded by the dynamic architect duo Ola Kilińska and Ola Leszczyńska. We create distinctive interiors and commercial spaces that are as functional as they are inspiring. Combining disciplines from interior design and strategy to product development and visual identity, we create concepts that unite practicality with beauty. Our spaces do more than work – they resonate, evoke emotion, and leave a lasting impression.
studio.heroImageAlt: Ola Kilińska and Ola Leszczyńska in the kool studio workspace

commercial.label: Commercial interiors
commercial.heading: Restaurants, concept stores, boutiques, hotels, services
commercial.description: In commercial spaces, design is a tool. We create visually cohesive places that support business and build brand value. We design expressive concepts that consciously shape the user experience. We know that first impressions are made only once.
scopeItems: analysis of assumptions in the context of the interior
defining the character, atmosphere and principles of the venue
support in the approval process with the sanitary inspectorate (Sanepid)
coordination of trades and author's supervision
commercial.sloganHeading: We create places that stay in memory
commercial.sloganText: Beyond functionality and compliance with regulations, our years of experience allow us to create spaces that reflect the brand's character, building its recognition.

details.cta: Explore the full offer for commercial interiors
details.projectDescription: We design commercial interiors that support the growth of brands. We combine strategy, branding and interior architecture, creating spaces that streamline business operations, build brand recognition and stay in customers' memory.
details.types: Restaurants / cafés / bars / boutiques / hotels / concept stores / fitness / SPA studios / offices
details.fitTitle: This project will be right for you when:
fitItems: You are opening a restaurant, café, hotel, shop or service venue and want to create a space consistent with your brand's character.
You are choosing or adapting premises and want to plan their function well before the fit-out begins.
You care about an interior that is comfortable in everyday use and well designed for both customers and staff.
You want to create a place that sets your brand apart and stays in customers' memory.
You are planning to refresh an existing venue without a full-scale rebuild.
You are planning to grow the brand and want solutions that can be rolled out across future locations.

stages.title: Brief, research and strategy
stages.text: We get to know how your business works, your target group, the character of the brand and the goals the space is meant to achieve. We analyse the location, competition and inspirations to create an interior that supports the company's growth and builds a coherent customer experience.

stages.title: Site survey and venue analysis
stages.text: We examine the potential and constraints of the space — carrying out a survey and analysing the technical condition, services and formal requirements. This way the key decisions are made on facts, before design work begins.

stages.title: Functional layout and zoning
stages.text: We design how the venue works — the arrangement of service zones, back-of-house and circulation. We test layout variants so the space runs smoothly for customers and staff alike, while meeting regulatory requirements.

stages.title: Visual concept
stages.text: We create an interior concept consistent with the brand's character — mood, materials, colours and light. Visualizations let you see the space before the fit-out begins and make further decisions consciously.

stages.title: Construction design
stages.text: We prepare detailed technical documentation — trade drawings, details, custom joinery and material solutions. A complete set of information that lets contractors price and deliver the project precisely.

stages.title: Specifications and quotes
stages.text: We prepare material and equipment specifications and support you in selecting trusted contractors. We help keep the investment budget and schedule under control.

stages.title: Author's supervision
stages.text: We watch over the fit-out — coordinating trades, answering contractors' questions and making sure the result matches the design, right up to opening day.

details.benefitsHeading:
What do you gain by choosing
a comprehensive interior design?

benefits.title: Conscious decisions
benefits.text: A comprehensive design lets you make the most important decisions before the fit-out begins. This reduces the risk of changes on site and helps manage the investment budget effectively.

benefits.title: A smoother fit-out process
benefits.text: Complete construction documentation and cross-trade coordination streamline cooperation with contractors. The investment runs more smoothly and the risk of errors is reduced.

benefits.title: A stronger brand
benefits.text: We design interiors that support the way a business operates and strengthen its image. A well-designed space builds brand recognition and creates an experience customers want to return to.

workBlurbs.delikatesy-dehesa: An Iberian delicatessen — terrazzo, dark joinery and red accents, with a bespoke visual identity.
workBlurbs.biuro-dobry-material: An office reflecting the brand's dynamic character — colour, large-format graphics and movable screens.
workBlurbs.winobar-lodz: A wine bar in a post-industrial hall — preserved brick ceiling and an intense orange bar. In collaboration with blsk.studio.

faqItems.q: When is the best time to approach a commercial interior architect?
faqItems.a: Ideally before signing the lease or buying the premises. We can then assess the potential of the space, its technical and formal constraints, and plan the budget realistically. If the venue is already chosen — the earlier we start, the more decisions are made before work begins.

faqItems.q: How much does the design of a restaurant, hotel or shop interior cost?
faqItems.a: The cost depends on the floor area, the scope of cooperation and the complexity of the project. We prepare each quote individually after a short brief — write to us and you will receive a concrete proposal of scope and price.

faqItems.q: How long does a commercial interior project take?
faqItems.a: Depending on the scale — from a few weeks for a refresh of an existing venue to several months for a comprehensive restaurant or hotel project. We agree the schedule at the start of the cooperation and run the project in stages.

faqItems.q: What kinds of commercial interiors do you design?
faqItems.a: Restaurants, cafés, bars, boutiques, hotels, concept stores, fitness and SPA spaces, and offices. Our portfolio includes a delicatessen, a wine bar, offices, a food hall and a hotel — see the selected works above.

faqItems.q: Does a restaurant design require approvals from the sanitary inspectorate?
faqItems.a: Yes — food service is subject to sanitary and technological requirements. We design layouts that comply with the regulations and support the investor through the approval process with the sanitary inspectorate (Sanepid) and certified experts.

faqItems.q: Can the interior design also cover branding and visual identity?
faqItems.a: Yes. We design interiors together with visual identity — logo, signage, printed materials or uniforms — so the brand stays coherent at every point of contact with the customer.

faqItems.q: Does the design take the assumed investment budget into account?
faqItems.a: Yes — the budget is one of the design assumptions from the very beginning. We select solutions and materials to fit the agreed frame, and specifications and quotes keep costs under control at every stage.

faqItems.q: Why is it worth hiring an interior architect for a restaurant, hotel or service venue?
faqItems.a: A commercial interior has to work for the brand — a well-designed space streamlines the team's work, builds recognition and influences customers' decisions. An experienced architect also helps avoid costly mistakes and navigate the formal requirements smoothly.

faqItems.q: Can an existing venue be refreshed without a full rebuild?
faqItems.a: Yes — we carry out refurbishments of existing commercial interiors. Often a change of colours, lighting, furniture and details is enough to give the interior a new character without a costly rebuild.

faqItems.q: Do you design interiors with brand growth and a future chain of venues in mind?
faqItems.a: Yes — we create concepts that can scale. We define the brand's rules and signature elements so that subsequent locations stay coherent while being tailored to each specific space.

faqItems.q: Where do you deliver commercial interior projects?
faqItems.a: We work across Poland — our projects include Wrocław, Łódź, Warsaw, Gdańsk and Ustronie Morskie. We are based in Wrocław, but location is not a constraint.

residential.label: Residential interiors
residential.heading: Premium apartments and houses
residential.description: We design private spaces thoughtfully, in dialogue with context and residents' needs. We consider daily comfort of use, durability of solutions, and unique approaches. The relationships between materials, colors and textures are important to us. We ensure that aesthetics stem from quality, not fleeting trends.
scopeItems: analysis of residents' needs and expectations
residential.sloganHeading: We create places you want to come back to
residential.sloganText: We guide every project from concept to precise technical documentation, allowing the idea to be translated into a calm and predictable realization. We have experience in both developer and secondary market construction, and we have contacts with verified contractors.

process.label: Comprehensive project
process.heading: We don't treat a project as a single stage. It's a process that requires consistency.
steps: Brief and analysis of needs and budget
process.bottomHeading: We manage the process comprehensively from initial assumptions to the final result.
process.bottomText: We spread decisions over time, from general to specific, which allows us to capture all nuances and avoid random choices. We organize the entire process by eliminating doubts and, thanks to our experience, we facilitate decisions. This allows for a calm realization and a result consistent with the assumptions. As part of a comprehensive project, we support you at every stage.

cookies.message: We use cookies for stats and ads.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: The controller of your personal data is kool studio, Zaporoska 83/15, Wrocław, Poland. For any data-related matters, write to hello@koolstudio.pl.
privacy.contactBody: When you write to hello@koolstudio.pl or send a brief via the contact form, we process the data you provide (name, email address, project details) solely to reply and discuss a potential collaboration; after sending a brief you receive an email confirmation. We keep correspondence for as long as the matter requires.
privacy.analyticsBody: With your consent we use PostHog (data processed in the EU) to understand how visitors use the site — which projects they view, how they find us, and how they move around the site (session recording: cursor movement and scrolling, never the content of text inputs). Without your consent we measure traffic anonymously only: no cookies and nothing stored on your device.
privacy.adsBody: If we run ad campaigns (e.g. on Instagram), with your consent we may use cookies that measure their effectiveness (e.g. Meta Pixel). Without consent these tools are never loaded.
privacy.rightsBody: You have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, port, and object to the processing of your data. You can also lodge a complaint with the Polish supervisory authority (UODO).
privacy.cookiesBody: You can change your cookie decision at any time — here or via the "cookies" link at the bottom of the page:

brief.heading: TELL US ABOUT YOUR PROJECT
brief.intro: A short brief helps us prepare for the conversation. Fill in as much as you can — the more we know, the better we can respond.
brief.requiredHint: Fields marked with an asterisk (*) are required.

budget.label: Approximate implementation budget (optional)
budget.help: If you'd like, give a rough figure.

priorities.label: Priorities
priorities.help: What should work better than it does today? Max. 3 points.

plansUrl.label: Link to plans or photos
plansUrl.help: A link to a cloud folder. No file upload.

brief.privacy: Your details go solely to hello@koolstudio.pl and are used only to reply to this enquiry.
